title: Iso42001GapAssessmentInput
description: Input payload for the ISO 42001 Gap Assessment skill. Requires an organisation description, AI portfolio in scope, and jurisdictions. Regulatory Mapping Section 2 output is strongly recommended to pre-populate applicable ISO 42001 clauses.

organisation_description.description: Description of the organisation — sector, size, AI use cases, and the governance programme context driving the assessment.

ai_portfolio.description: List of AI systems in scope for the AIMS. Each entry should name the system, its function, and its deployment status.

jurisdictions.description: Jurisdictions in scope for the assessment. Shapes regulatory overlay applied in Section 2 and Section 9.

regulatory_mapping_output.description: Strongly recommended. Full output from skills/regulatory-mapping/. Section 2 (Applicable Governance Frameworks) pre-populates ISO 42001 clauses and Annex A controls identified as relevant. Section 6 (Control Requirements) maps to specific Annex A controls. Without this input, clause identification proceeds from scratch.

control_mapping_output.description: Recommended. Full output from skills/governance-control-mapping/. Section 3 (Control Coverage Classification) identifies which controls are already designed and covered — prevents duplication in the Gap Register.

existing_documentation.description: Documentation the organisation already holds that may satisfy ISO 42001 requirements — ISO 27001 certificate, SOC 2 report, existing AI policy, prior gap assessment results, board-level AI ethics statement.

target_certification.description: The target outcome of the assessment. Third-party certification applies the strictest evidence standards. Shapes ARS severity calibration and Section 9 audit readiness verdict.

industry.description: Industry sector. BFSI triggers SR 11-7 / PRA SS1/23 model risk overlay on top of standard clause assessment.

current_maturity_baseline.description: Prior assessment results if available. Used to measure progress between assessments rather than starting from baseline zero.
